titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Bertha Stoneman papers, 1894-1945.
titlestmt.author: Compiled by E. Engst

titlepage.titleproper: Guide to the Bertha Stoneman Papers,1894-1945.
titlepage.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections Cornell University Library

did.head: DESCRIPTIVE SUMMARY
did.physdesc: Correspondence, speeches, student notebooks, thesis, photographs, and poetry.
did.repository: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library
did.abstract: Correspondence, speeches, student notebooks, thesis, photographs, and poetry of Bertha Stoneman. Also, Huguenot College yearbooks, University of Toronto centenary programs (with a reference to Livingston Farrand), and pamphlets about South Africa.

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Bertha Stoneman graduated from Cornell, Class of 1894, and received a Doctor of Science degree in botany in 1896. She joined the faculty of Huguenot College in Wellington, Cape Colony (South Africa), a women's college. From 1928 until her retirement in 1933, she served as president of the college.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, speeches, student notebooks, thesis, photographs, and poetry of Bertha Stoneman. Also, Huguenot College yearbooks, University of Toronto centenary programs (with a reference to Livingston Farrand), and pamphlets about South Africa.

did.unittitle: Publications including student notebooks, thesis, and poetry
Photographs (latgely unidentified) of Bertha Stoneman, friends, and students at Huguenot College (?)

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Bertha Stoneman papers, #4230. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
